input.prompt:
I'm building a chat messaging screen in .NET MAUI targeting .NET 10. When the
keyboard opens, it covers my text input at the bottom of the screen. In .NET 9
I used WindowSoftInputModeAdjust.Resize but that doesn't seem to work anymore.
How do I make the input bar move up when the keyboard appears?

input.initialPrompt:
I'm building a chat messaging screen in .NET MAUI targeting .NET 10. When the
keyboard opens, it covers my text input at the bottom of the screen. In .NET 9
I used WindowSoftInputModeAdjust.Resize but that doesn't seem to work anymore.
How do I make the input bar move up when the keyboard appears?

data.reasoningText:
The user is asking about a .NET MAUI issue where the keyboard covers the bottom text input on Android. They mention that `WindowSoftInputModeAdjust.Resize` no longer works in .NET 10. Let me provide guidance based on what I know about this topic.

In .NET MAUI / Android, the `WindowSoftInputModeAdjust.Resize` was deprecated in Android 11 (API 30+) and the recommended approach shifted to using `WindowInsets` / edge-to-edge APIs. In .NET 9, there were compatibility shims, but in .NET 10, the behavior may have changed.


For .NET 10, the modern approach involves using MAUI's built-in `KeyboardAutoManagerScroll` feature, which automatically handles scrolling content up when the keyboard appears, or alternatively using `AdjustPan` instead of `AdjustResize` depending on the desired behavior. The edge-to-edge and window insets APIs are also part of the solution for proper keyboard handling on newer Android versions. In the Android platform code, I'd listen for keyboard insets and adjust the view accordingly, or leverage MAUI's built-in `KeyboardAutoManagerScroll` which handles this automatically for scroll view scenarios. For a chat screen, the pattern is straightforward: position the input bar at the bottom, and when the keyboard opens, both the input bar and message list shift up. Since `adjustResize` is deprecated at the OS level on Android 30+, the practical approach in .NET MAUI is to use the auto-scroll feature or implement custom `WindowInsets` handling for more control.
